description: A subscription to a never-completing observable is created and never torn down, leaking across navigations.
body: Failure mode: ngOnInit subscribes to a router, store, or websocket stream that never completes and there is no ngOnDestroy teardown, so each time the component mounts another live subscription accumulates and old ones keep firing. Detection signals: a side effect runs more times than mounted components; memory grows as the user navigates; a bare .subscribe() has no async pipe, takeUntilDestroyed, or destroy Subject. Do not leave a long-lived subscribe untorn-down. Safer fallback: bind the stream with the async pipe so Angular manages teardown, or add takeUntilDestroyed()/a destroy Subject completed in ngOnDestroy, and test that emissions stop after destroy.
